# packages/icons — Agent Guidelines

## Auto-Generated Files (DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY)

The following files are auto-generated by the build pipeline. Manual edits will be overwritten on the next `generate` run.

| Path | Generator |
|------|-----------|
| `src/icons/*.tsx` | `scripts/generate-components.ts` |
| `src/stories/*.stories.tsx` | `scripts/generate-stories.ts` |
| `src/index.ts` | `scripts/generate-exports.ts` |

To change icon components or stories, modify the **source** (SVG files or generator scripts), then regenerate:

```bash
npx tsx scripts/generate-components.ts  # regenerate components
npx tsx scripts/generate-stories.ts     # regenerate stories
npx tsx scripts/generate-exports.ts     # regenerate barrel exports
```

## SVG Originals

`svg/{name}/{variant}.svg` files are the source of truth. To change an icon's appearance:

1. Edit the SVG file under `svg/`
2. Run `npx tsx scripts/optimize-svgs.ts` (SVGO)
3. Run `npx tsx scripts/generate-components.ts`

Do NOT edit `src/icons/*.tsx` directly.

## icon-manifest.json

`scripts/icon-manifest.json` maps icon names to external node IDs. The values are opaque identifiers from the design source — do not fabricate or guess them.

## Storybook

Stories are auto-generated alongside components. Do not create or edit `.stories.tsx` files manually. Story format, args, and decorators are controlled by `scripts/generate-stories.ts`.

## Build Pipeline Order

The full pipeline runs 5 steps in sequence (`scripts/generate.ts`):

1. `fetch-figma.ts` — Download SVGs (requires `.env` credentials)
2. `optimize-svgs.ts` — SVGO optimization
3. `generate-components.ts` — SVG to React components
4. `generate-exports.ts` — Barrel export (`src/index.ts`)
5. `generate-stories.ts` — Storybook stories

Steps 2-5 can be run independently. Step 1 requires network access and `.env` configuration.
